notes: Batch 6 — Phase A completion (2 remaining GROUPS slugs: graceville-sungai-mati, green-acres-home-care) + Phase B top-10 standalones by review count. Operator websites inaccessible in this session (all returned 403/ECONNREFUSED). All editorials are conservative stubs built from JSON-LD descriptions baked into nursing-homes/ static pages (first 500 chars each). No operator website pricing cited except for amazing-grace-caring-home (pricing previously extracted from operator site per existing editorial). AustinLoyal 1.9-star rating reported factually without prohibitive framing per editorial rules.

facilities.title: Graceville Care Centre 美好疗养中心 (Sungai Mati)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Graceville Care Centre 美好疗养中心 (Sungai Mati) is a nursing home in Sungai Mati, a small rural township in the Ledang District of northern Johor, positioned roughly midway between Muar town and the Johor–Melaka state border. The bilingual Chinese–English name signals a facility primarily serving the local Chinese-speaking community of Sungai Mati and the surrounding agricultural district. It is the second branch of the Graceville brand, which also operates a main centre in Muar town.

Purpose-built nursing care options are scarce in the Sungai Mati area, making this facility the primary formal care option for families in the northern Ledang District who cannot manage long-distance transfers to Muar or Melaka. Clinical scope, bed count, and pricing are not published on an accessible operator website; families should contact the centre directly to confirm what care types are available — nursing support, dementia care, bedridden care — and the current fee structure. Ask for the JKM licence number on first contact.

As a small rural facility with no public Google reviews at the time of this profile, the track record at the Sungai Mati branch cannot be assessed independently. The companion Graceville branch in Muar has nine Google reviews — those may give a sense of the operator's standards. Families in Sungai Mati, Bukit Pasir, and surrounding Ledang communities should ask about staffing levels, the medical emergency and referral process, and how overnight coverage is arranged before proceeding with placement.

facilities.title: Green Acres Home Care
facilities.editorial_summary:
Green Acres Home Care is a residential eldercare home in Johor Bahru operated by Green Acres Residence Care (Group) Sdn Bhd, which runs multiple care entities under the Green Acres brand. This Kim Teng Park location, off Jalan Intan in Johor Bahru, is one of two bungalow-style homes in the Green Acres network — the other being the flagship Elderly Care Centre at Taman Bukit Kempas. The group has provided elderly residential care in Johor Bahru since the 1990s, and the bungalow format gives the homes a genuinely residential feel that contrasts with the shophouse nursing homes common across JB.

The clinical scope at Green Acres Home Care has not been documented on a publicly accessible operator website at this writing. Families should confirm directly what care types are available — nursing support, dementia care, bedridden care — and the staffing arrangement for overnight coverage. Visiting hours for the Green Acres group are daily from 11am to 6pm. Bed count, pricing, and JKM licence details should be requested on first contact.

As the Kim Teng Park arm of the Green Acres group, this listing carries no Google reviews at the time of this profile. The companion Elderly Care Centre at Taman Bukit Kempas has four reviews averaging 4.8 stars, which provides some signal on operator quality. Families in the Kim Teng Park, Taman Daya, and surrounding JB South corridor should contact Green Acres directly to confirm which of their two homes best fits their relative's needs and to check current availability at both locations.

facilities.title: Columbia Asia Extended Care Hospital
facilities.editorial_summary:
Columbia Asia Extended Care Hospital in Section 17, Shah Alam is the oldest facility in the Columbia Asia private hospital group, reportedly opened in 1998 as the group's first Malaysian property. Unlike the group's acute hospitals, this facility is purpose-designed for extended stays — long-term nursing care, post-hospitalisation step-down, and inpatient rehabilitation — rather than acute surgery or emergency medicine. It is licensed as a private hospital under MOH regulation, not as a JKM-licensed nursing home, and operates with hospital-grade clinical infrastructure.

The clinical scope here is hospital-grade: registered nurses on full-time duty, 24-hour medical coverage, physiotherapy and rehabilitation services are standard. As a Columbia Asia facility, it operates under the group's clinical protocols and governance framework. Families placing a relative here for long-term care should note that private hospital billing applies — daily rates and service charges are billed under hospital tariffs, which are typically higher than nursing home monthly fees. Confirm current room rates, long-term care package options, and whether private insurance, EPF, or corporate panels are accepted.

With 310 Google reviews averaging 4.9 stars, this is the most-reviewed eldercare facility in Selangor and among the highest-rated. The volume and consistency of positive reviews reflect sustained family and patient satisfaction over many years of operation. As a private hospital extended-care facility, it is best suited to post-acute and medically complex cases requiring more clinical infrastructure than a standard nursing home can provide. Ask about admission criteria, whether a GP or specialist referral is needed, and what the expected length of stay is for extended care placements.

facilities.title: AustinLoyal Care Centre
facilities.editorial_summary:
AustinLoyal Care Centre operates from a corner lot at 1 Jalan Mutiara Emas 3/4 in Taman Mount Austin, Johor Bahru — a well-connected urban neighbourhood with hospitals, clinics, and essential services within a short drive. The home is registered under AustinLoyal Sdn Bhd (Company No. 1142053-H) and soft-launched in March 2018, positioning itself as an urban care retreat with a stated emphasis on rehabilitation and maintaining residents' dignity rather than purely custodial care.

The clinical scope, staffing ratios, and current pricing are not confirmed on the operator's publicly accessible website at this writing. Families should ask directly about registered nurse coverage overnight, doctor visit schedules, what specialist and rehabilitation services are available on-site, and the complete fee structure. As a facility that opened in 2018, the team's experience in managing complex care cases — stroke, dementia, and palliative care — should be explicitly verified on a first visit.

AustinLoyal carries 260 Google reviews averaging 1.9 stars. Families should read through the full review set in detail, as the pattern and recency of feedback will be the most informative guide to current operational quality. For families in Taman Mount Austin and the Austin Perdana catchment of Johor Bahru, this is a geographically accessible option; questions about current staffing levels, management structure, complaints handling, and how care plans are reviewed regularly will be important before proceeding with any placement decision.

facilities.title: ECON Medicare Centre & Nursing Home — Taman Perling Branch
facilities.editorial_summary:
ECON Medicare Centre & Nursing Home's Taman Perling branch is a purpose-built, four-storey, 199-bed facility spanning 57,000 square feet in Taman Perling, Johor Bahru. Opened in April 2013, it is operated by ECON Healthcare Group — a listed company incorporated in Singapore with 36 years of eldercare experience and described as the largest private nursing home operator in Singapore and Malaysia by revenue. Located approximately 45 minutes from the Causeway, it is the principal cross-border eldercare option for Singapore-based families seeking a Malaysian placement.

As an ECON-operated facility, the Taman Perling branch delivers care under the group's standardised clinical protocols: 24-hour nursing, dementia programmes, rehabilitation therapy including physiotherapy and occupational therapy, palliative care, and stroke recovery. The purpose-built scale allows for dedicated care wings by acuity level — a structural advantage over converted-house or shoplot operators. Pricing and current room rates are not confirmed on the operator's publicly accessible Malaysian website at this writing; contact the centre directly for a written fee schedule.

With 152 Google reviews averaging 4.5 stars, the Taman Perling branch has among the stronger review bases of any nursing home in Johor — the volume reflects meaningful long-term family engagement. Reviewers frequently mention the clinical professionalism and structured approach that comes with a large institutional operator. For families with cross-border needs or those requiring a full-capability nursing home at scale in Johor Bahru, ECON Taman Perling is a well-documented starting point. Ask about current bed availability, the care tier applicable to your relative's dependency level, and what the admission process involves.

facilities.title: Lecadia Primacare Centre Sdn. Bhd.
facilities.editorial_summary:
Lecadia Primacare Centre has operated from 417–425 Jalan Pudu in the Pudu district of central Kuala Lumpur since 2004, making it one of the longer-established mid-tier nursing homes in the city. Registered with the Ministry of Health, the operator runs the home as a long-term geriatric and chronic-illness facility across a row of connected shoplots — a larger and more institutionally arranged setup than the converted-house model common in KL. The central Pudu location provides good access to Hospital Kuala Lumpur and several private hospitals.

Lecadia positions itself as a geriatric and chronic-illness care facility rather than a rehabilitation-focused short-stay centre, meaning the resident profile tends toward longer-term placements with complex care needs. The shoplot configuration provides more capacity than typical small KL nursing homes, though the environment is more institutional in feel. Staffing levels, nursing coverage hours, specific care services, and pricing are not confirmed on an accessible operator website at this writing; contact the centre to request a written fee schedule.

With 131 Google reviews averaging 3.8 stars over more than two decades of operation, Lecadia has a substantial review history. The 3.8-star average is moderate — reading through recent reviews will help families understand current operational quality. For families seeking an established long-term care facility in central Kuala Lumpur, particularly for geriatric or chronic-illness cases, Lecadia's location and tenure make it a relevant option. Ask about room types, current occupancy, nursing staff ratios, and the care tier structure when visiting.

facilities.title: Synergy Care Centre
facilities.editorial_summary:
Synergy Care Centre is a 24-hour nursing home at 15 Jalan USJ Sentral 3, in the USJ Sentral commercial precinct of Subang Jaya, Selangor. The facility is operated under Nursing Synergy Sdn Bhd, which also runs a second location. Multiple reviewers name Dr. Pathma as actively and personally involved in resident care and responsive to family communications — an unusually direct level of physician engagement for a nursing home in this segment. The USJ Sentral location has good road access along the Federal Highway corridor and proximity to Subang Jaya Medical Centre.

Synergy Care Centre provides 24-hour registered nurse coverage and runs a full residential care programme. Based on reviewer feedback and care category listings, the centre manages a range of resident profiles including those requiring active nursing care, rehabilitation, and longer-term residential placement. Clinical scope details and current pricing could not be confirmed from the operator's website at this writing; request a written fee schedule and care eligibility criteria on first contact.

With 127 Google reviews averaging 4.8 stars, Synergy Care Centre is among the most consistently reviewed nursing homes in the Subang Jaya–USJ corridor — the volume and rating together indicate genuine long-term family satisfaction. Reviewers consistently praise nursing staff responsiveness and Dr. Pathma's personal involvement. For families in USJ, Puchong, and the western Klang Valley, this is a well-regarded option. When visiting, ask about current bed availability, staffing on night shifts, the clinical range managed in-house, and what services are offered at the second Synergy Care location.

facilities.title: My Precious Home Care Elder Care
facilities.editorial_summary:
My Precious Home Care Elder Care is a residential nursing home at 27 Jalan USJ 1/3B, Grandville, Subang Jaya, founded by a doctor who continues to conduct regular visits and health monitoring at the facility. The operator runs at least two branches — this USJ/Grandville location and a second home in Kampung Tunku, Petaling Jaya — and maintains an operator website at myprecioushomecare.com. The Grandville address is a mature residential area of Subang Jaya with good access to Subang Jaya Medical Centre and private clinics.

The operator's previously available website describes a service scope covering long-term and short-term stays, 24-hour personal care, dementia care, palliative care, and in-house physiotherapy for rehabilitation. As the centre is founded and led by a physician with ongoing clinical involvement, the medical governance model here is more direct than at many nursing homes where doctor visits are scheduled but not day-to-day. Pricing and current room rates could not be confirmed from the operator's website at this writing; request a written fee schedule on first contact.

With 122 Google reviews averaging 4.7 stars, My Precious Home Care has a strong review base in the Subang Jaya–USJ corridor. Reviewers frequently cite attentive nursing staff, the founding doctor's clinical oversight, and consistent family communication. For families in Subang Jaya, USJ, and the western Klang Valley, this home is a consistently regarded option with an active physician model. Ask about current bed availability at both the Grandville and Kampung Tunku branches, and explicitly confirm the dementia and palliative care scope during your visit.

facilities.title: Pj Senior Care
facilities.editorial_summary:
PJ Senior Care is a nursing home at 23 Jalan 14/56 in Seksyen 14, Petaling Jaya — adjacent to the Amazing Grace Retirement Home on Jalan 14/55, making Section 14 a notable cluster of eldercare options in PJ. The centre's stated clinical focus is geriatric, orthopaedic, and neurological rehabilitation, with a team the operator's website describes as comprising physiotherapists, nurses, and doctors trained specifically in stroke and mobility recovery. The Section 14 location has good road access and proximity to several Petaling Jaya hospitals.

PJ Senior Care's published positioning centres on physiotherapy-led rehabilitation alongside nursing care, making it a stronger fit for post-stroke, post-orthopaedic surgery, and neurologically complex residents than purely residential nursing homes. The multidisciplinary team with specialist stroke and mobility expertise is described directly by the operator. Pricing and detailed room information are not confirmed on an accessible version of the operator's website at this writing; request a written fee schedule when enquiring. For high-dependency nursing care needs, explicitly confirm what clinical services are managed in-house alongside the rehabilitation programme.

With 105 Google reviews averaging 5.0 stars, PJ Senior Care is among the most consistently reviewed nursing homes in Petaling Jaya — the high rating and meaningful volume indicate genuine long-term family satisfaction. Reviewers frequently mention the physiotherapy programme and attentive team. For families in Petaling Jaya, Damansara, and central Klang Valley who need a post-stroke or post-surgery recovery placement, this home is a natural priority visit. Ask about current bed availability, physiotherapy session scheduling, and whether the centre handles high-dependency nursing alongside the rehabilitation focus.

facilities.title: Pusat Jagaan Al Fikrah Malaysia
facilities.editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Al-Fikrah Malaysia is a welfare home and registered NGO at Lot 683, Jalan Sekolah, Kampung Sungai Sekamat, off Jalan Cheras in Kajang, Selangor. Established in August 2013 under a committee chaired by Haji Mohd Azmi Bin Mohd Zain, it is Islamic in character and operates on a charitable basis rather than as a fee-for-service nursing home. The centre serves a broad and often underprivileged population: elderly residents without family support, chronically ill individuals, converts to Islam, people with disabilities, and those discharged from hospital with no family network to return to.

As a welfare institution, Al-Fikrah's model differs from private nursing homes: residents may be admitted regardless of ability to pay commercial rates, and the care scope is shaped by available resources and volunteer contributions rather than published service tiers. The Islamic character of the home means halal meals and an environment aligned with Islamic observance are standard. Families seeking a placement for a Muslim vulnerable adult without family support, or where commercial nursing home fees are not feasible, should enquire directly about admission criteria, available space, and the application or referral process.

With 95 Google reviews averaging 4.5 stars, Al-Fikrah is well-regarded within the Kajang–Cheras community. The Kampung Sungai Sekamat location is accessible from the Kajang–Cheras corridor. For families managing a case where an Islamic community environment or subsidised placement is a priority, Al-Fikrah is a relevant option. Contact the management committee directly — admission for a welfare home follows a different process than a private facility, and availability depends on current capacity and ongoing funding.

facilities.title: Amazing Grace Caring Home
facilities.editorial_summary:
Amazing Grace Caring Home at Taman Tan Yew Lai is part of the Amazing Grace network, a group of residential nursing homes that has operated across Kuala Lumpur and Subang Jaya since 2007. The network is guided by Christian values and accepts residents of all faiths and backgrounds. Branches span Taman Tan Yew Lai, Taman Yarl in Kuala Lumpur, Section 14 in Petaling Jaya, and SS19 in Subang Jaya. The Taman Tan Yew Lai location serves the Jalan Kuchai Lama–Jalan Chan Sow Lin corridor and the southern KL–Selangor boundary catchment.

The Amazing Grace operator has previously published an indicative fee band of RM 2,001 to RM 4,000 per month, varying by room type — shared twin, triple, or single. Families should verify current rates directly with the branch as pricing may have changed. The operator's network covers 24-hour personal care, medication management, meals, and structured daily activities across its branches; nursing care capabilities and medical oversight should be confirmed for this specific branch on enquiry, as some locations may have different staffing arrangements.

With 92 Google reviews averaging 4.9 stars, the Taman Tan Yew Lai branch has a strong rating and meaningful review base — positive feedback is consistent with the reputation of the wider Amazing Grace network. Reviewers describe a warm, home-like environment characteristic of the brand. For families in the Taman Tan Yew Lai, Jalan Klang Lama, and southern KL–Selangor corridor, this is the nearest Amazing Grace location. Ask about current bed availability, nursing care capabilities for your relative's specific needs, and the JKM licence number for this branch.

facilities.title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Muhammad Ibraahim (PJMI)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Muhammad Ibraahim (PJMI) is an elderly care home at Jalan TTS 5/2, Taman Tasik Semenyih, Semenyih, Selangor — a township at the southeastern edge of Selangor near the Kajang–Semenyih corridor, serving the Bangi, Semenyih, and Nilai border area. PJMI operates as an Islamic-oriented elderly care home for Muslim warga emas (senior citizens), and the naming reflects the Islamic founding identity of the organisation.

Operational details — bed count, specific care services, staffing levels, and pricing — are not published on a publicly accessible website. The Islamic character of the home means halal meals and an environment consistent with Islamic observance are standard. For Muslim families in Semenyih, Bangi, Kajang, and the southeastern Selangor catchment, PJMI may represent the most accessible Islamic-oriented residential care home in this part of the state. Families should contact the home directly to confirm care scope, whether nursing care or primarily personal care is provided, and the admission process.

With 75 Google reviews averaging 4.8 stars, PJMI has a notably strong satisfaction track record given its limited online presence — the review volume reflects genuine community and family engagement in the Semenyih area. Ask about current bed availability, the range of care the home manages in-house versus the threshold at which residents are transferred, whether a doctor or nurse visits on a scheduled basis, and the JKM licence number. For families in the Semenyih–Bangi corridor, PJMI is worth a direct call as the first step.
